_comment: Per-package coverage thresholds — the mechanical AGENTS.md §11 gate (Phase 21.5). Each `min` is keyed to a band: new-package 80%, conformance 85%, cli-tooling 70%. An override band (harness-override, subprocess-override) carries a `reason` documenting why the package cannot hit its product band hermetically. Adding a new Go package requires a new entry here OR an `exempt` line — coveragecheck fails on an unconfigured measured package by design.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/internal/bu.reason: Orchestrates `go build` and Vite subprocesses; the toolchain-failure and cross-compile-matrix branches cannot be driven hermetically (Phase 20 documented this). Held to the CLI-tooling band, not the new-package band.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/internal/cl.reason: Phase 29 — renders the published CLI reference page from the cobra command tree (D-140). Walks the in-tree command set; held to the CLI-tooling band.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/internal/sk.reason: Phase 29 — SKILL.md format validator for the §19 hygiene rule (D-138). Pure-fn / read-only; held to the new-package band.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/internal/in.reason: Runs the installed server's localhost boot check as a subprocess; the boot-failure and host-profile-detection branches are not hermetically reachable. Held to the CLI-tooling band.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/internal/ru.reason: Supervises the built server child process; the signal/exit-race branches cannot be driven deterministically in a hermetic suite (Phase 20 documented this). Held to the CLI-tooling band.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/internal/va.reason: Phase 18 set a 75% target for the validate engine — above the 70% CLI-tooling floor; kept as the package's bar.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/runtime/sto.reason: Conformance harness — a test-helper package. Its statements are exercised when the sqlite/inmem drivers run the suite, not by its own thin self-test; self-coverage sits below every product band by construction.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/runtime/tas.reason: Conformance harness — a test-helper package. Its statements are exercised when the task-store drivers run the suite; self-coverage sits below every product band by construction.

github.com/hurtener/dockyard/templates/a.reason: The analytics-widgets builtin: //go:embed + the substitution table that materialises it (Phase 24, decision D-128). Held to the new-package band — the unit test (builtin_test.go) drives Materialise + substitutionsFor + titleCase; the integration test drives the full real cycle end to end.

Demonstration handlers shipped by the analytics-widgets template (Phase 24, decision D-124). Synthetic data; a developer who scaffolds the project swaps each handler body for a real source. Held above the 70% CLI-tooling floor (the Phase 24 plan target) — the surface is exemplary, not production-critical for the framework itself.

The approval-flows builtin: //go:embed + the substitution table that materialises it (Phase 25, decision D-128). Held to the new-package band — builtin_test.go drives Materialise + substitutionsFor + titleCase; the Phase 25 integration test drives the full real scaffold-and-build cycle end to end.

Demonstration handlers shipped by the approval-flows template (Phase 25, decision D-134). Unlike the synchronous analytics-widgets handlers, these are inherently tasks.Engine-driven — the Handler happy paths kick off run* goroutines that the engine pumps via SupplyInput. Their happy-path coverage lives in test/integration/phase25_approval_flows_test.go, which drives the full input_required lifecycle through a real engine; in-package unit tests cover the edge cases (no-engine fallback, terminal builders for approved/rejected/declined, summarisers, decoders, marshallers, finalise helper). The Phase 21.5 coverage-gate override mechanism (D-092) exists precisely for packages whose happy paths require integration coverage rather than mocked-engine duplication.
